content.problemStatement:
Consider all matrices with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM) containing only integers between 000 and K−1K-1K−1 (inclusive). For each such matrix AAA, let's form a sequence L1,L2,…,LN+ML_1, L_2, \ldots, L_{N+M}L1​,L2​,…,LN+M​:


For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), LiL_iLi​ is the maximum of all elements in the iii-th row of AAA.
For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), LN+iL_{N+i}LN+i​ is the maximum of all elements in the iii-th column of AAA.
Find the number of different sequences formed this way. Since this number may be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 505050
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 505050
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200200200
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 200200200
KKK is the same for all test cases
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of different sequences, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
